name: ci-green
description: Drive a PR's CI checks to green: diagnose each failure, push a fix, repeat until all checks pass.
rubric: Every required CI check on the PR reports success (verified against the live PR, not assumed).
The PR is mergeable (no conflicts with the base branch).
No CI job, test, or check was disabled, skipped, or marked flaky to get there.
Each fix pushed this loop was reproduced and verified locally before pushing, where local reproduction was possible.
fixPrompt: Each iteration: (1) Fetch the PR's current check status and the logs of every failing job. (2) Reproduce the failure locally where possible using the matching command from the validation matrix in .github/harness/HARNESS.md. (3) Fix the root cause, run the local equivalent until green, commit and push to the PR branch. (4) Wait for CI to report (prefer event subscriptions over polling; never busy-wait with sleep). Converges when all required checks are green and the PR is mergeable.
guardrails: Never disable, skip, or mark-flaky a CI job or test to converge — fix the cause.
A rebase/re-run without a code change counts as an iteration and is allowed at most once for suspected infrastructure flakes.
If a failure is real but out of scope for the PR, report it instead of fixing it in-loop.
onExhausted: Stop. Report each still-failing check with its log excerpt, the fixes attempted per iteration, and whether the failure looks like PR code, pre-existing breakage, or CI infrastructure.
